vulnerability.reference: IoT devices deployed in physically accessible locations (homes, factories, streets) offer attackers unlimited side-channel collection opportunities.
vulnerability.generated: End users rely on physically accessible IoT devices whose AES implementations leak side-channel information without any hardware or software countermeasures.

impact.reference: Compromised industrial sensor networks feed falsified data into SCADA systems, triggering unsafe physical processes.
impact.generated: Unauthorized decryption of industrial sensor data and IoT communications enables mass data exfiltration and malicious device control across critical systems.

dose_response.reference: Exact SASCA enables more accurate key recovery, which in physically accessible IoT devices exposes encryption keys, leading to compromised sensor networks and mass recalls.
dose_response.generated: Exact inference in SASCA via probabilistic circuits, through end users' physically accessible, unprotected IoT devices, triggers accurate key recovery from side-channel leakage, leading to unauthorized decryption, mass data exfiltration, and malicious device control.
